records.ety: [OE. atiren to array, dispose, arrange, OF. atirier; (L. ad) + F. tire rank, order, row; of Ger. origin: cf. As. tier row, OHG. ziarī, G. zier, ornament, zieren to adorn. Cf. Tire a headdress.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Attired (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Attiring.]

defs: To dress; to array; to adorn; esp., to clothe with elegant or splendid garments.
quotes: Finely attired in a robe of white.
With the linen miter shall he be attired.

defs: Dress; clothes; headdress; anything which dresses or adorns; esp., ornamental clothing.
quotes: I 'll put myself in poor and mean attire.
Can a maid forget her ornament, or a bride her attire?

defs: The antlers, or antlers and scalp, of a stag or buck.
The internal parts of a flower, included within the calyx and the corolla.
